skill.name: ai-research-explore
skill.description: Rigor Explore compatible skill for exploring novel deep learning research candidates. Use when the researcher has chosen the task family, dataset, benchmark, evaluation method, and SOTA references, and wants auditable candidate exploration on top of current_research with idea gating, fair comparison, and governed experiments.
skill.summary: Explore and rank novel deep learning research candidates with auditable repo understanding, idea gating, and fair comparison workflows.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate research exploration tool that uses subprocess for running internal pass scripts, hashlib for cache identity digests, and network calls to public research APIs (GitHub, arXiv, DOI) for metadata resolution. The static analyzer flagged 601 potential issues, but most are false positives from regex-based pattern matching. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings refer to hashlib usage for non-security purposes (cache deduplication), not authentication. Subprocess calls execute internal pass scripts with controlled arguments, not user-controlled shell injection. Network access is limited to well-known research metadata providers. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Subprocess execution of internal scripts
high_findings.description: scripts/orchestrate_explore.py uses subprocess.run to invoke internal pass scripts with sys.executable. Arguments are constructed from internal function calls, not user-controlled shell input. This is a legitimate orchestration pattern for multi-pass research workflows.

medium_findings.title: Network requests to public research APIs
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to GitHub API, arXiv API, and DOI resolvers to fetch research metadata. These are legitimate public endpoints used for source resolution. No credential exfiltration detected - the code only reads optional API keys from environment for authenticated GitHub requests.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for optional API tokens
medium_findings.description: scripts/lookup/providers/optional_provider.py reads environment variables for optional API tokens (e.g., GitHub token). This is standard practice for authenticated API access and does not constitute credential theft.

low_findings.title: Hashlib usage for cache identity (not authentication)
low_findings.description: The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings from the static analyzer refer to hashlib usage in cache_store.py and other files for generating stable content digests for cache deduplication. This is not a security-sensitive use case - the hashes are used for record identity, not for authentication or integrity protection.

low_findings.title: Base64 decode of GitHub API README content
low_findings.description: scripts/lookup/providers/github_provider.py decodes base64-encoded README content from the GitHub API. This is the standard format the GitHub API returns file content in, not an obfuscation technique.

content.user_title: Explore Novel Deep Learning Research Candidates
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to systematically explore and rank novel deep learning ideas with scientific rigor. This skill provides auditable candidate exploration with idea gating, fair comparison, and governed experiment workflows on top of current_research.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes repository structure to understand the research codebase before candidate exploration
Generates and ranks research candidate ideas with explicit gating criteria and score breakdowns
Maps candidate ideas to source references from arXiv, DOI, and GitHub repositories
Evaluates execution feasibility of proposed research candidates with bounded resource estimates
Maintains auditable SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG and COMPARABILITY_REPORT artifacts for each campaign
Supports cache-first source lookup with local curated literature and optional web providers
limitations: Does not provide autonomous discovery or guarantee global benchmark completeness
Novelty claims are hypotheses, not proofs - requires human verification against literature
Requires an existing current_research anchor (branch, commit, checkpoint) before use
Network-dependent lookups require optional API tokens for authenticated access

use_cases.title: Systematic Idea Exploration
use_cases.description: A researcher wants to explore several candidate improvements to their current model. They use this skill to generate ranked ideas with feasibility scores and scientific changelogs.

use_cases.title: Fair Comparison Workflows
use_cases.description: A research team needs to compare candidate approaches against their SOTA reference with controlled variables and auditable evidence. The skill enforces frozen evaluation sources and comparability reports.

use_cases.title: Literature-Grounded Ideation
use_cases.description: A PhD student wants to brainstorm novel research directions grounded in existing literature. The skill maps candidate ideas to arXiv and DOI sources for context.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Candidate Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a current_research branch exp/my-baseline with a trained checkpoint. Help me explore 3 candidate improvements using the ai-research-explore skill. Focus on architecture variations that preserve the current evaluation protocol.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use with an existing baseline

prompt_templates.title: Campaign with SOTA Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a research_campaign with current_research at commit abc1234, benchmark GLUE, evaluation_source from my eval pipeline, and sota_reference paper 2301.00000. Generate candidate ideas for attention mechanism improvements with budget 4 GPU-hours.

prompt_templates.title: Source-Mapped Idea Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Map my current idea 'sparse attention with learned routing' to relevant sources from arXiv and GitHub. Then generate 2 derivative candidates that preserve the routing mechanism but vary the sparsity pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Feasibility and Fidelity Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the execution_feasibility and implementation_fidelity passes on my top-3 ranked candidates. Report which ones are decomposable into auditable units and which need checkpoint discussion.

output_examples.input: Explore candidates for improving my transformer baseline on GLUE
output: Generated 3 candidate ideas ranked by expected gain and feasibility
Candidate 1: Sparse attention with fixed pattern (feasibility: 0.85, expected gain: medium)
Candidate 2: Learned routing with top-k selection (feasibility: 0.72, expected gain: high)
Candidate 3: Layer-wise learning rate decay variant (feasibility: 0.90, expected gain: low)
Sources mapped: 4 arXiv papers, 2 GitHub repos
Decomposable into 3 auditable units: routing module, top-k selector, loss integration
Evaluation risk: low (uses existing eval pipeline)
Recommendation: proceed to bounded smoke test

best_practices: Always establish a durable current_research anchor (branch, commit, or checkpoint) before starting exploration
Freeze the task family, dataset, benchmark, and evaluation source before generating candidate ideas
Use cache-first source lookup and prefer local curated literature over open-ended web searches
Write SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG and COMPARABILITY_REPORT artifacts to maintain auditable evidence
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for trusted reproduction - use ai-research-reproduction instead
Do not skip the explicit exploration authorization step or proceed without a current_research anchor
Do not present exploratory gains as verified improvements or claim global benchmark completeness
Do not silently choose between top candidates if the implementation cannot be decomposed into auditable units

faq.question: What is the difference between ai-research-explore and ai-research-reproduction?
faq.answer: ai-research-explore is for candidate-only exploration with auditable evidence and idea gating. ai-research-reproduction is for trusted, verified reproduction of published results.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee novel research discoveries?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides a structured workflow for exploring candidates, but novelty and significance remain hypotheses until verified against literature and ablation evidence.

faq.question: What inputs are required to start a research campaign?
faq.answer: You need a current_research anchor (branch, commit, or checkpoint), an explicit task family, dataset, benchmark, evaluation method, and at least one SOTA reference.

faq.question: Can the skill run experiments autonomously?
faq.answer: The skill can plan and gate experiments, but actual execution requires explicit human authorization and uses bounded resource budgets defined in the campaign policy.

faq.question: What artifacts does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill writes candidate-only outputs to analysis_outputs, sources, and explore_outputs directories, including SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md and COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: Network access is optional. The skill prefers local curated literature (such as Zotero) and cached sources. Public API lookups (arXiv, DOI, GitHub) are used as fallback when local sources are insufficient.
